scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run compile && npm run bundle
scripts.compile: tsc -b && tsc --project tsconfig.mcp.json && npm run bundle:mcp && node scripts/syncToolsToPackageJson.js
scripts.bundle: npm run bundle:extension && npm run bundle:mcp

contributes._languageModelTools_WARNING: DO NOT EDIT languageModelTools — auto-generated from gsl/mcp/toolDefinitions.ts by scripts/syncToolsToPackageJson.js

languageModelTools.name: gsl_download_script
languageModelTools.userDescription: Downloads a GSL script from a game server instance.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Downloads a GSL script from the specified server instance and saves it to a local file. Returns the file path. Use this when the user wants to read, review, or inspect a version of a script on any server instance.

instance.description: Which server instance to fetch from. Defaults to 'dev'.

languageModelTools.name: gsl_diff_script_across_instances
languageModelTools.userDescription: Compares a GSL script between two server instances.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Downloads a GSL script from two server instances and returns a unified diff showing exactly what lines differ between them. Use this when the user wants to compare, diff, or check differences between two versions of a script across server instances. The diff reads as: baseInstance (---) compared against compareInstance (+++).

baseInstance.description: Reference instance (--- side of diff). Defaults to 'prime'.

compareInstance.description: Instance to compare against base (+++ side of diff). Defaults to 'dev'.

context.description: Number of unchanged context lines around each change. Defaults to 3.

ignoreWhitespace.description: If true, ignore leading/trailing whitespace when comparing lines.

languageModelTools.name: gsl_compile_check
languageModelTools.userDescription: Compiles a GSL script and returns errors/warnings.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Use this tool whenever the task is to check, compile, or validate GSL script compiler errors/warnings. It compiles a local GSL script file on the development server and returns compiler output, including line-level errors and warnings, so the model can diagnose and fix compilation problems. The file is uploaded to the game-specific compilation slot (script 24661 in GemStone or 16224 in DragonRealms). Use this tool when writing scripts or verifying GSL syntax.

filename.description: Path to the .gsl file to compile. Prefer an absolute path (e.g. '/home/user/scripts/S02017.gsl'). A relative path like 'S02017.gsl' is resolved against the configured download directory.

languageModelTools.name: gsl_get_current_author
languageModelTools.userDescription: Returns the current GSL author from the login config file.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the current GSL author value, per the configuration file. Use this when generating changelog entries or script metadata that needs the canonical author identity. Format: <AbbreviatedRealName>/<CharacterName>.

languageModelTools.name: gsl_get_room_data
languageModelTools.userDescription: Retrieves room data from the game server.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Loads the segment for a given room ID, sends the /sr command to the game server, and returns the full room data output, including room name, varfields aka properties, flags, and directions. This does NOT show objects currently in the room, except those explicitly linked in the room description texts. Existence IDs referenced in room text (e.g. $+$-516105D) are PID'd (permanent) and therefore negative — always preserve the negative sign when using them with other tools such as `gsl_get_existence_data`. Can target any server instance. Defaults to Dev.

roomId.description: The room ID to look up.

instance.description: Which server instance to query. Defaults to 'dev'.

languageModelTools.name: gsl_get_existence_data
languageModelTools.userDescription: Retrieves existence (item/object) data from the game server.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Sends the /se (show existence) command to the game server for a given existence ID and returns the full existence data output, including the item name, varfields aka properties, flags, and location. Negative IDs are permanent IDs (PID'd) — these are stable references that persist across unload/reload cycles. Positive IDs are temporary and may change. Can target any server instance. Defaults to Dev.

existenceId.description: The existence ID to look up. Negative IDs are permanent (PID'd) and stable across reloads.

instance.description: Which server instance to query. Defaults to 'dev'.

languageModelTools.name: gsl_get_player_varfields
languageModelTools.userDescription: Retrieves player varfields and flags from the game server.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Sends the /svf command to the game server for a given player name and returns varfield and flags output. Use this when the user wants to inspect a character's current state — skills, stats, properties, and flag bits.

playerName.description: The player name to look up.

verbosity.description: Controls output verbosity. 'NoTables' (default) omits table varfields. 'SkipDefaults' omits fields at default values. 'Full' returns everything.

instance.description: Which server instance to query. Defaults to 'dev'.

languageModelTools.name: gsl_slash_agent_command
languageModelTools.userDescription: Runs a /agent subcommand on the game server.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Sends a `/agent <subcommand> [args...]` command to the game server and returns the full output. The /agent verb is a programmable dispatcher on the game server that supports various subcommands for querying game data. Run with no arguments to list available subcommands. Run with just a subcommand name (no args) to see its usage. Can target any server instance. Defaults to Dev. See S24792.gsl for implementation.

command.description: The subcommand and its arguments, without the /agent prefix (e.g. 'vftable PlayerName tableName 0 5'). Omit to list all available subcommands.

instance.description: Which server instance to query. Defaults to 'dev'.

languageModelTools.name: gsl_get_script_ss_metadata
languageModelTools.userDescription: Retrieves script metadata (/ss) from the game server.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Sends the /ss (show script) command for a given script ID and returns the raw script metadata output. This is the best way to identify when a script was last updated, by whom, and whether scripts are in sync across servers — compare the output for the same script ID across different instances (e.g. prime versus dev). Warning: script owner property is typically not maintained and is weak signal — better to look at who has touched the script recently by viewing the script's changelog, found in the script file itself.

scriptId.description: The script ID to look up.

instance.description: Which game instance to query script data for. Maps to game codes: dev=GS4D, prime=GS4, shattered=GSF, platinum=GS4X, test=GST. Defaults to 'dev'.

languageModelTools.name: gsl_get_verb_data
languageModelTools.userDescription: Retrieves verb metadata from the game server.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Sends the /sv (show verb) command for a given verb name and returns the raw verb metadata output, including the script number that handles the verb, access level, parse code, verb flags, and modification history. Can target any server instance. Defaults to Dev. Warning: the 'Owned by' field is typically not maintained and is a weak signal — to identify who actually works on a verb's script, look at the 'Last modified by' field or the script's changelog found in the script file itself.

verb.description: The verb name to look up (e.g. 'sit', '/mongen', 'quest', 'incant').

instance.description: Which server instance to query. Defaults to 'dev'.

languageModelTools.name: gsl_get_table_metadata
languageModelTools.userDescription: Retrieves global table metadata from the game server.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Sends the /sl (show table) command for a given table ID and returns the table metadata, including the table description, creator, access level, dimensions (X/Y/Z), total size, and table type flags. Does not work on varfield tables (e.g. on players/exists). Can target any server instance. Defaults to Dev.

tableId.description: The global table ID to look up.

instance.description: Which server instance to query. Defaults to 'dev'.

gsl.automaticallyDownloadScripts.description: If enabled, scripts will be automatically downloaded when their matchmarker definitions are needed.

gsl.enableScriptSyncChecks.description: Enable to check sync status when downloading scripts.

gsl.disableLoginAttempts.description: Enable to permanently disable login attempts from various actions in the editor.

gsl.alwaysEnabled.description: Enable to always have the Download/Upload buttons.

gsl.displayGameChannel.description: Enable to display the Game channel when the editor starts.

gsl.downloadPath.description: Path to save downloaded scripts.  Window users: use double slashes when specifying, e.g. C:\\GemStone\\Scripts

gsl.downloadToWorkspace.description: If enabled, will attempt to save downloaded scripts in the current open root workspace folder.

gsl.fileExtension.description: File extension to use for downloaded scripts.

gsl.scriptPollingDelay.description: Set the interval for how often the extension checks if the local script is up-to-date with its remote version.  A higher delay can be useful in bandwidth-constrained situations such as on an airplane.

gsl.loginConfigFile.description: Path to the JSON login config file for game server instances. Defaults to ~/.gsl/loginConfig.json if not set. Run 'GSL: User Setup' to create.

themes.label: GSL In The Sky With Naos
